nodeTutorial.title: Node setup guide
nodeTutorial.heroDesc: A personal node keeps your transactions private — no third party sees what you send or receive.
nodeTutorial.s1Body: When you use a public API, your wallet addresses and transaction history are sent to external servers. A personal node keeps all data on your own infrastructure, giving you full privacy and financial sovereignty.
nodeTutorial.s2Body:
This app connects to the Mempool API. Self-host it via Docker:

git clone https://github.com/mempool/mempool
cd mempool/docker
docker compose up -d

Default URL after setup:
http://your-server:8080/api
nodeTutorial.s3Body: Umbrel is a one-click personal server OS. Install it on any computer or Raspberry Pi, then install the Mempool app from the Umbrel app store. The connection URL appears in the app dashboard.
nodeTutorial.s4Body: RaspiBlitz and Start9 are Raspberry Pi-based Bitcoin node platforms. Both support a self-hosted Mempool instance. Find the Mempool URL in your node's dashboard after installing the Mempool app.
nodeTutorial.s5Body:
URL — full address of your Mempool instance, e.g. https://node.example.com/api
Port — leave empty if already included in the URL
Auth token — optional bearer token if your instance requires authentication
nodeTutorial.s6Body: After entering the URL, tap 'Test connection'. A green 'Connected' status confirms the node is reachable and the network matches your wallet. 'Network mismatch' means your node runs on a different network.
nodeTutorial.s7Body: On a local network, plain HTTP is fine. For remote access, always use HTTPS and set an auth token. For maximum privacy, access your node over a VPN or Tor hidden service to avoid exposing your IP address.

manageNodes.emptyDesc: Add a personal Bitcoin node to route your transactions privately.
manageNodes.publicFallbackDesc: If all nodes fail, fall back to the public Mempool API
manageNodes.info: Nodes are tried in priority order. Lower priority number = higher preference. When using a personal node, your transaction data never leaves your infrastructure.

safeMode.activeDesc: All blockchain calls are routed through your personal nodes.
safeMode.inactiveDesc: Connecting via public API. Enable to use your own nodes.
safeMode.info: Safe mode routes all blockchain traffic through your personal Bitcoin nodes, ensuring maximum privacy and independent verification.

walletPolicy.title: Wallet Policy
walletPolicy.heroDesc: Everything you need to know about creating and importing wallets safely.
walletPolicy.s1Body: When creating or importing a wallet, the app runs three steps automatically: setting up your keys, discovering existing accounts on the blockchain, and syncing all your transaction data.
walletPolicy.s2Body: Your 12-word seed phrase is the only key to your wallet. It is stored encrypted on your device and never transmitted anywhere. Anyone who has your seed phrase has full access to your funds.
walletPolicy.s3Body: When importing an existing wallet, the app scans the blockchain to find all your accounts and history. It checks up to 3 consecutive unused addresses per account (BIP44 gap limit) before concluding that no more accounts exist.
walletPolicy.s4Body: After discovery, the app downloads your UTXOs, transactions, and balances from the Mempool API. You can sync again at any time by tapping the sync button on the home screen.
walletPolicy.s5Body: You can create separate accounts (called 'origins') to organise your funds — for example: Default, Savings, Trading. Each account has its own receive and change addresses derived from the same seed phrase.
walletPolicy.s6Body: A passphrase is an extra secret combined with your seed phrase to unlock a completely different wallet. If you forget it, your funds become permanently inaccessible. Only use this feature if you fully understand the risk.

addressPolicy.title: Address Policy
addressPolicy.heroDesc: How the wallet generates, tracks, and protects your Bitcoin addresses.
addressPolicy.s1Body: The wallet never reuses receive addresses. Each payment uses a fresh address, making it significantly harder for anyone to track your full transaction history on the blockchain.
addressPolicy.s2Body: Your wallet manages two kinds of addresses: receive addresses (shared with senders) and change addresses (for leftover funds after a send). Both are derived from the same seed phrase but follow separate derivation paths.
addressPolicy.s3Body: An address starts as 'fresh', becomes 'reserved' when selected, then 'received' once funds arrive. After spending, it becomes 'spent' and is never suggested for receiving again. This lifecycle protects your privacy.
addressPolicy.s4Body: The app always keeps at least 3 fresh receive and 3 fresh change addresses ready for each account. New addresses are derived automatically as existing ones are used, so you never run out.
addressPolicy.s5Body: When spending any UTXO from an address, all UTXOs from that same address are included in the same transaction. This prevents dust accumulation and reduces traceability of your funds.
addressPolicy.s6Body: The app selects the most efficient combination of UTXOs for each transaction. Frozen UTXOs are never included. You can manually freeze or unfreeze any UTXO from the UTXO management screen.
addressPolicy.s7Body: Private keys are never stored in the address database. They are derived from your seed only at the moment of signing a transaction, then immediately discarded from memory.

accountPolicy.title: Account Policy
accountPolicy.heroDesc: How accounts work, how addresses are derived, and how your funds stay isolated and private.
accountPolicy.s1Body: An account is a logical partition of your wallet — a dedicated set of addresses for a specific purpose (savings, trading, etc.). All accounts share the same seed phrase but use different BIP44 account indexes, so their funds are completely isolated on the blockchain.
accountPolicy.s2Body:
This wallet follows BIP84, which produces native SegWit addresses starting with bc1q. These offer the lowest possible transaction fees and the broadest wallet compatibility.

Mainnet: m/84'/0'/accountIndex'/chain/index
Testnet: m/84'/1'/accountIndex'/chain/index
accountPolicy.s3Body:
Each account uses a unique accountIndex in the BIP44 derivation path. Your default account is index 0. New accounts increment this index sequentially.

Default:   m/84'/0'/0'
Account 2: m/84'/0'/1'
Account 3: m/84'/0'/2'
accountPolicy.s4Body:
Within each account, addresses are split into two chains. The wallet manages both automatically — you only ever share receive addresses.

chain 0 (receive): m/84'/0'/accountIndex'/0/index
chain 1 (change):  m/84'/0'/accountIndex'/1/index
accountPolicy.s5Body: The wallet always keeps at least 3 fresh receive and 3 fresh change addresses ready per account. New addresses are derived automatically as existing ones are used. The gap limit is 20 — scanning stops after 20 consecutive unused addresses during wallet import.
accountPolicy.s6Body: Once an address has been spent, it is permanently retired. It will never be suggested for receiving again. Change always goes to a fresh change address, never back to the originating address. This prevents blockchain observers from linking your transactions.
accountPolicy.s7Body: Private keys are never stored in the address database. Only public data is kept — derivation paths, balances, and transaction references. Your private key is derived from your seed only at the moment of signing, then immediately discarded from memory.
